metadata.name: real_estate_ca
metadata.description: Auto-generated dataset from prompt (12 requested, 12 generated)

extracted_rules:
1. Validate all numeric fields (age, postal_code digits, phone digits) against the specified format and range; if invalid, leave unchanged and document the issue.  
2. Postal codes must match their country’s standard format (e.g., 5 digits for US, A1A 1A1 for Canada); if the format is unrecognizable, keep the original and note uncertainty.  
3. Standardize state/province names to their full, unabbreviated forms and country names to full, official names (e.g., “Canada” not “CA” or “USA”).  
4. Expand street abbreviations only in the address field (St → Street, Ave → Avenue, Rd → Road, Blvd → Boulevard) unless the surrounding text indicates a proper noun that should stay unchanged.  
5. Convert names and city names to Proper Case; if a spelling correction is uncertain, retain the original value and record the reason.  
6. If a record’s country is missing but an IP address is available, infer the country from the IP; if the country cannot be confidently determined, leave the country blank and note the limitation.  
7. For postal codes that are full (6 characters, e.g., M6H 1E7), never modify them; use them as authoritative and only verify via web search.  
8. For partial FSA postal codes (3 characters), attempt a web search to resolve the full code; if only one confident match is found, complete the code and record high confidence; if ambiguous, set the code to “FSA ?” and flag for review.  
9. If a postal code is missing, perform a web search using the street address, city, and province; populate the code only if a single confident result is returned; otherwise set the field to “N/A”.  
10. Apply the Canadian cross‑province sanity check: if the first letter of the postal code does not match the stated province/city, flag a “CROSS‑PROVINCE MISMATCH” and set municipality to “N/A”, halting further processing for that record.  
11. The municipality field must reflect the real‑estate neighbourhood, not the administrative city or district; determine it via web search using the full postal code or resolved address, and record the confidence level.  
12. If any field cannot be confidently determined or verified (postal code, municipality, country, state/province, phone format), leave the original value and document the uncertainty in validation_notes.  
13. All date fields (if present) must be formatted as ISO 8601; if the format is incorrect or ambiguous, retain the original value and note the issue.  
14. General cleaning (typos, inconsistent casing) should be performed only when the correction is certain; otherwise, maintain the original data and record the decision.  
15. Validation notes must accompany every change, detailing what was altered, the rationale, and a confidence level (HIGH/MEDIUM/LOW).

test_cases.description: All fields already conform to standards (happy path)
evaluation_criteria: cleaned_data matches raw_data exactly, exclude validation_notes if present in comparison

test_cases.description: Name spelled incorrectly, case issue in city, postcode partially missing
test_cases.expected_behavior: Name corrected to Proper Case; city corrected to Proper Case; country expanded from CA to Canada; state_province expanded from AB to Alberta; postal_code left unchanged with note about unrecognized format
evaluation_criteria: postal_code unchanged with note about unrecognized format

test_cases.description: Missing country but IP available inferring Canada
test_cases.expected_behavior: Country inferred as Canada from IP; state_province expanded to British Columbia; phone format validated; other fields unchanged
evaluation_criteria: validation_notes include IP inference with HIGH confidence

test_cases.description: no postal code provided, IP address lookup for country / province
test_cases.expected_behavior: country corrected from Can to Canada (using IP 184.70.44.78 to confirm if needed); state_province expanded from MB to Manitoba; postal_code left null with note that lookup is required; municipality preserved pending lookup
evaluation_criteria: postal_code remains null with validation note that lookup is required

test_cases.description: Postal code missing but address unique, web search yields multiple matches
test_cases.expected_behavior: postal_code set to N/A due to ambiguous search; municipality left as is with LOW confidence note

test_cases.description: Cross‑province mismatch detected in Canadian postal code and province
test_cases.expected_behavior: Cross‑province mismatch flagged; municipality set to N/A; processing halted for this record

test_cases.description: Invalid age non‑numeric, phone format incorrect for country
test_cases.expected_behavior: age left unchanged with note; phone left unchanged with note about format uncertainty; other fields validated
evaluation_criteria: validation_notes for both fields indicating uncertainty

test_cases.description: Edge case: complete Canadian postal code with whitespace variation
test_cases.expected_behavior: Postal code left as full, whitespace corrected if needed; no modification; validation notes confirm authoritative code

test_cases.description: Phone number missing for country, cannot infer; leave unchanged
test_cases.expected_behavior: Phone left null; validation_notes note lack of data; other fields verified

test_cases.description: Ambiguous municipality needing web search, yet only one match found
test_cases.expected_behavior: municipality confirmed as West End with HIGH confidence; validation_notes reflect source

test_cases.description: Address contains proper noun abbreviation not to be expanded
test_cases.expected_behavior: Address kept exactly; no abbreviation expansion; validation notes note decision
evaluation_criteria: validation_notes explain no expansion due to proper noun
